parsed_findings.chain_steps: Exploit ORDER BY injection to alter the SELECT result set; then pivot by using injected UNION/time-based/stacked queries to enumerate and extract sensitive user data.
parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can read or modify data by changing the query semantics, potentially escalating to full database compromise depending on DB privileges and driver settings.
parsed_findings.proof: At line 7, `q = f'SELECT ... ORDER BY {sort} {direction}'` directly concatenates request-derived values into executable SQL without parameterization.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a request with `sort` set to an allowed value but `dir` set to a payload such as `asc;--` (or DB-specific comment/stacked-query syntax) to break out of the ORDER BY clause and influence the resulting SQL.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: User-controlled `sort` and `direction` are interpolated into an SQL string used by `ORDER BY`, enabling SQL injection beyond the `allowed` check and allowing crafted payloads to alter the query structure.
